title: sambucus nigra subsp. peruviana
intro: Sambucus nigra subsp. peruviana, also known as the Peruvian black elderberry, is a subspecies of the Sambucus nigra plant, a flowering shrub native to South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Peruvian black elderberry include reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, as well as supporting immune function and reducing inflammation. Peruvian black elderberry is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and supplements, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
